name: Standard Push-Up
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands just outside shoulder width, body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows to roughly 45 degrees from your torso.
Stop when your chest is a few inches from the floor.
Press back up to full arm extension.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: The foundational bodyweight pressing movement — hands under shoulders, body in a straight line.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your core braced the whole rep.
Don't let hips sag or pike up.
Full lockout at the top.
commonMistakes: Flaring elbows straight out to the sides
Sagging hips
Partial range of motion

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions: Set up in a push-up position with hands close together under your chest, thumbs and index fingers forming a diamond shape.
Keep elbows tracking close to your body as you lower your chest toward your hands.
Press back up to full extension, squeezing the triceps at the top.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A close-grip push-up with hands forming a diamond shape, shifting emphasis onto the triceps.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep elbows tucked close, not flared.
Keep core braced, avoid sagging hips.
commonMistakes: Hands too far forward
Flaring elbows out

name: Knee Push-Up
instructions: Kneel on a mat or soft surface, hands under shoulders, body in a straight line from head to knees.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Press back up to full arm extension.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A push-up performed from the knees instead of the toes, reducing the load for beginners building toward a full push-up.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep a straight line from head to knees, don't let hips sag.
Control the descent.
commonMistakes: Letting hips sag or pike up
Partial range of motion

name: Pike Push-Up
instructions: Start in a downward-dog-like position: hands and feet on the floor, hips raised high, forming an inverted V.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Press back up to the starting position, straightening your arms.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A push-up performed with hips raised high, angling the torso closer to vertical to bias the shoulders over the chest.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips high throughout.
Keep elbows tracking forward, not flared.
Look slightly forward, not straight down.
commonMistakes: Letting hips drop toward the floor
Flaring elbows too wide

name: Split Squat
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance, one foot forward and one foot back, both feet planted on the floor.
Lower your body straight down by bending both knees until your rear knee nearly touches the floor.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
movementPatternDescription: A stationary lunge — both feet planted in a staggered stance throughout the set, building single-leg strength and balance.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your torso upright.
Keep most of your weight on the front leg.
Front knee should track over your toes, not cave inward.
commonMistakes: Taking too short or too long a stance
Letting the front knee cave inward

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on your forearm, elbow directly under your shoulder.
Lift your hips off the floor so your body forms a straight line from head to feet.
Hold this position, keeping your core braced and hips level.
Maintain the hold for the target duration, then switch sides.
movementPatternDescription: A static hold on one side of the body, building lateral core (oblique) strength and stability.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep hips lifted and stacked, don't let them sag or rotate.
Keep your neck neutral.
commonMistakes: Letting hips drop toward the floor
Rotating the torso forward or back

name: Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, hands lightly supporting your head.
Curl your shoulders and upper back up off the floor, flexing through your abdominals.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower back down with control.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A classic spinal-flexion core exercise, curling the upper torso toward the pelvis.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Lead with your chest, not your chin.
Keep your lower back on the floor.
Exhale as you curl up.
commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with your hands
Using momentum instead of controlled ab contraction

name: Sissy Squat
instructions: Stand holding onto something stable for balance, feet hip-width apart.
Rise onto the balls of your feet and lean your torso back as you bend your knees, letting them travel forward.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your quads, keeping your body in a straight line from knees to head.
Reverse the motion, driving through your quads to return to standing.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: An advanced quad-isolation squat where the knees travel far forward and the torso leans back, maximizing quad stretch and tension.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep a straight line from knees to shoulders throughout.
Move slowly, this is not a fast exercise.
Hold onto support until you're confident with balance.
commonMistakes: Bending at the hips instead of staying rigid
Going too fast and losing control at the bottom

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Grip a pull-up bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder width, palms facing away from you.
Hang with arms fully extended.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar, driving your elbows down and back.
Lower back down with control to a full hang.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: The classic vertical pulling movement — hanging from a bar and pulling the chin above it.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Avoid swinging or kipping.
Drive elbows down, not just bending the arms.
Fully extend at the bottom each rep.
commonMistakes: Using momentum
Partial range of motion
Shrugging instead of engaging the lats

name: Pistol Squat
instructions: Stand on one leg, extending the other leg straight out in front of you.
Lower your hips down and back, bending the standing knee, while keeping the extended leg off the floor.
Descend as low as your mobility allows, ideally until your hips are near your heel.
Drive through your standing heel to return to standing.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
movementPatternDescription: A single-leg squat performed with the non-working leg extended straight out in front — an advanced test of unilateral strength, balance, and mobility.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Keep your standing heel planted, don't let it lift.
Keep your torso as upright as your mobility allows.
Use a wall or doorframe for balance assistance while learning.
commonMistakes: Heel lifting off the floor
Falling forward at the bottom

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding onto a wall or chair for balance if needed.
Rise up onto the balls of both feet as high as possible.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing the calves.
Lower back down with control until your heels nearly touch the floor.
Repeat for the recommended repetitions.
movementPatternDescription: A two-legged bodyweight calf raise performed standing, the foundational calf-building movement.
rirDescription: Stop shy of technical failure to preserve form.
romDescription: Move through the fullest range your joints and stability allow.
fatigueDescription: Moderate fatigue, recoverable within the session.
skillDescription: Requires some body awareness and control.
stabilityDescription: Requires core and joint stabilization throughout.
coachingCues: Rise straight up without letting your ankles roll.
Pause at the top for a full squeeze.
Lower all the way down for a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Bouncing instead of controlled reps
Partial range of motion
